event: light pain in the right ear
swelling and redness of the skin behind the right ear
sent to the nearest Ear-Nose-Throat Service
treated with antibiotics [Amoxicillin 500 mg three times a day (ter die sumendum – TDS)], painkillers and anti-inflammatory drugs
but severe headache, anorexia, and high fever appeared after a few days
marked right facial and periorbital swelling
visual acuity of 7/12 on her right eye
intraocular pressures in both eyes are normal
no clinical evidence of optic disc swelling
left eye seemed to be fine
displayed a wide spectrum of eye movements
complained of pain on right eye movement
no clinical evidence of right optic nerve compression
renal and liver functions within normal limits
tests for vasculitis, human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), and diabetes were all negative
coagulation profile and urinalysis were both normal
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain revealed non-opacification of the right cavernous sinus
received empirically high-dose intravenous (iv) Ceftriaxone [2 g twice a day (bis in die – BD)] and anticoagulation with Enoxaparin (8000 IU BD)
developed shortness of breath, and reduced oxygen saturation
unilateral homogeneous opacification on right lobar lung with air bronchograms specific to a right lobar pneumonia
referred to the Intensive Care Unit (ICU)
antibiotics were changed to Amikacin 20 mg/kg once a day (omne in die – OD) + Piperacillin–Tazobactam 4.5 g TDS
symptoms resolved in the coming days
transferred back to our Service after seven days
control contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance angiography (CE–MRA) was performed
persistence of the lacunar image in the right cavernous sinus
discharged two weeks later, on oral anticoagulant – Acenocoumarol
if a follow-up CT venogram will indicate resolution of the thromboses after three months, the treatment will be stopped
